stories.title: EU’s top court finds Hungary’s anti-LGBTQ+ law in breach of key values
stories.summary: ECJ says law passed in 2021 is discriminatory and ‘contrary to the identity of the union’, in early test for new PM The EU’s highest court has found Hungary’s anti-LGBTQ+ law to be discriminatory, stigmatising and in breach of basic democratic values, setting up an early test for the incoming government when it takes power next month. In a wide-ranging judgment, the European court of justice said the 2021 law that bans content about LGBTQ+ people from schools and primetime TV was at odds with a

stories.title: EU to cut Venice Biennale funding over Russia’s participation, Kallas says
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday that the bloc intends to cut funding to the Venice Biennale following a decision by the art exhibition to readmit Russian artists to its 2026 edition. “While Russia bombs museums, destroys churches and seeks to erase Ukrainian culture, it should not be allowed to exhibit its own,” Kallas said after a meeting of EU foreign ministers. “Russia’s return to the Venice Biennale is morally wrong, and the EU intends to cut its funding.” Kallas made t

stories.title: EU members reject push to suspend association agreement with Israel
stories.summary: EU foreign ministers on Tuesday rejected a push to suspend the bloc’s association agreement with Israel following a debate that exposed the bloc’s deep divisions regarding the Middle East. In the lead-up to the April 21 meeting of the Foreign Affairs Council in Luxembourg, Spain, Ireland and Slovenia signed a letter last week to EU top diplomat Kaja Kallas accusing Israel of breaching the agreement that underpins the bloc’s relationship with Israel. But the ministers ultimately decli

stories.title: The great unblocking? EU foreign ministers eye sweeping changes as Orbán exits
stories.summary: LUXEMBOURG — In a room long shaped by stalemate and frustration, the mood among EU foreign ministers on Tuesday was notably lighter. Ministers meeting in Luxembourg for the first time since Viktor Orbán’s electoral defeat said the prospect of new leadership in Budapest had injected fresh hope into talks that for years had been weighed down by Hungarian obstruction. From aid for Ukraine to sanctions against violent West Bank settlers and the EU’s stalled efforts to bring in new countr

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Decision on EU’s €90bn loan for Ukraine ‘due in next 24 hours’ after Zelenskyy says oil pipeline repaired – as it happened
stories.summary: EU’s foreign policy chief, Kaja Kallas, says there is ‘new momentum’ after Hungarian election as Ukrainian leader says Druzhba pipeline can resume operations German foreign minister Joseph Wadephul also makes it very clear that he is relieved with the change of government in Hungary, calling it “a breath of fresh air” and a promise of hope for Ukraine. He urged Hungary to drop its “unusual blockade” for policies for Ukraine “as quickly as possible,” pointing to what he argued was a clear pro-Eur

stories.title: Albanians are being unfairly demonised in illegal immigration debate | Letter
stories.summary: Albania’s ambassador to the UK, Uran Ferizi, takes exception to media reports that he says selectively focus on the criminality of Albanians • Report: Albanians in UK scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, says ambassador These are challenging times in Britain. Economic pressure has a long history of finding a foreign face to blame. For several years now, that face has been Albanian – caricatured by certain media outlets and politicians in ways that are neither fair nor, frankly, very&n

stories.title: Albanians in UK scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, says ambassador
stories.summary: In a letter to the Guardian , Uran Ferizi criticises ‘obsession’ with demonising Albanians Albanians in Britain are paying the price in schools and workplaces of being scapegoated by rightwing media and politicians, the Albanian ambassador has said. Uran Ferizi also criticised Shabana Mahmood, the home secretary, for comments in parliament where she singled out Albanians when discussing problems with immigration. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.title: North Carolina officials to charge adults who allegedly ‘stood by’ teen fight that became mass shooting
stories.summary: Two teenagers were killed and five others wounded in Winston-Salem after pre-planned fight in park Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox North Carolina authorities say they are pursuing criminal charges against adults who allegedly “stood by” a scheduled fight among teenagers that led to a mass shooting that killed two people and wounded five others on Monday. “We’re sending the message that if you stand by, encourage, aid or abet our juveniles in delinque

stories.title: Trump says US likely to resume bombing Iran as ceasefire nears end
stories.summary: The US president rejects extending truce as Tehran warns of response and negotiators head to Islamabad Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox JD Vance’s planned trip to Islamabad, where he was expected to lead talks with Iran about a nuclear deal to end the war, was put on hold on Tuesday after Iran failed to respond about its participation, according to two people familiar with the matter. The vice-president was scheduled to depart on Tuesday morning for a

stories.title: Zelenskyy says Druzhba oil pipeline to Europe repaired
stories.summary: A multibillion euro aid package from the EU had been blocked over access to the pipeline, which brings Russian oil to countries like Hungary and Slovakia.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Picasso’s Guernica is the ultimate emblem of the horrors of war. It has no place in Spain's partisan squabbles | María Ramírez
stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. After more than four

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: SZELEKTOR26: tripla lemezen az új magyar zenei generáció
stories.summary: Felpörög a hazai underground: színre lép az új generáció, májusban érkezik a Telekom Electronic Beats SZELEKTOR26 tripla válogatáslemeze, addig pedig hétről hétre jönnek a nyertes előadók. Ezúttal paks2 van soron: ő a CLUB album egyik legerősebb trackjének alkotója.

stories.title: Magyar Péter már kedden délután fogadja az iskolaigazgatókat az irodájában
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője délután négy órakor találkozik az igazgatókkal. Kedden emellett tíz miniszterelnökkel és államfővel, és Kelemen Hunorral, az RMDSZ elnökével is egyeztet.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Robert Ficóval tárgyalt telefonon, a Beneš-dekrétumokra hivatkozó jogszabály hatályon kívül helyezését kérte tőle
stories.summary: Abban állapodtak meg, hogy az Európai Tanács következő ülésén személyesen folytatják a tárgyalásokat Brüsszelben.

stories.title: Magyar Színházi Társaság: Az indulat beszűkíti a tisztánlátást, inkább valódi párbeszédre van szükség
stories.summary: A társaság hétfői közgyűlésén arról beszéltek, a színház mostantól nem lehet hatalmi logikák eszköze.

stories.title: Orbán quietly sidesteps his last EU summit after years of envisioning major battles at each one
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán is leaving the body of EU heads of state and government without a proper farewell on account of his duties related to the transfer of power, which will conveniently allow him to avoid answering questions about the Szijjártó-Lavrov phone calls.

stories.title: A fővárosban bevált fegyelmezett vezetőként, most egy tizennégyszer nagyobb feladatot kap
stories.summary: Bujdosó Andreát a Tisza fővárosi frakcióvezetőjeként szigor jellemezte. A Fidesz trükkjeire azzal válaszolt, hogy a kormánypárt nem a városban élők és a magyar emberek problémáival foglalkozik, ha pedig személyeskedő sértegetés kezdődött, akkor ő is beleállt a dologba. Most egy 141 fős frakciót kell egyben tartania, ami nagyon különböző hátterű emberekből áll.

stories.title: Robert Fico: A Beneš-dekrétumok kérdésében alapvetően eltérő álláspontot képviselünk
stories.summary: A szlovák miniszterelnök is posztolt a Magyar Péterrel folytatott telefonbeszélgetésről.

stories.title: Péter Magyar asked Robert Fico to revoke legislation referencing Beneš Decrees
stories.summary: The two politicians spoke on the phone and agreed to continue their discussion in person in Brussels during the upcoming meeting of the European Council.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Garanciát kaptam arra, hogy az RMDSZ a jövőben tartózkodni fog a magyar pártpolitikai küzdelembe való beavatkozástól
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője Kelemen Hunorral, az RMDSZ elnökével személyesen egyeztetett kedden. Beszéltek a levélszavazásról, és a későbbi szavazások szabályainak felülvizsgálatáról is.

stories.title: Hungarian law equating gays with pedophiles violates EU law, CJEU rules
stories.summary: If the Hungarian state does not make changes to the law which was adopted in 2021, another lawsuit may follow – this time involving a monetary penalty.

stories.title: „Nagy csalódás az erdélyi emberekben” – az őt érő fenyegetések miatt mondta le erdélyi turnéját Balogh Levente, a Szentkirályi tulajdonosa
stories.summary: Az üzletember szerint azért érték fenyegetések, mert egyesek úgy értelmezik, hogy elárulta a magyar embereket, amikor kimondta, hogy nem a Fideszt támogatja.

stories.title: „Tartson ki, Elnök Úr!” – aláírásokat gyűjt a Fidesz-frakció Sulyok Tamás pozícióban tartásáért
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt akarja, hogy a köztársasági elnök mondjon le. A Fidesz gyorsan megragadta az alkalmat, és ellenpólust képez.

stories.title: Évekbe telik feltölteni az iráni háborúban elhasznált amerikai fegyverkészleteket, amit Kína elégedetten nyugtázhat
stories.summary: Az iráni háború vészesen csökkentette az USA már amúgy is elégtelen készleteit olyan fegyverekből, amiket egy Tajvan miatti esetleges konfliktushoz kellett volna tartalékolni Kínával szemben. Próbálják felpörgetni a gyártást, de ebbe Peking bármikor bekavarhat a ritkaföldfémek exportjának korlátozásával.

stories.title: Az Erste-vezér megkönnyebbült a választás eredményétől, másnap még a kokárdát is kitűzte
stories.summary: A Jelasity Radován által vezetett bankot Orbán a halál vámszedőjének nevezte, Nagy Márton pedig nem látta szívesen az országban.

stories.title: Zelenszkij: Befejeződtek a Barátság kőolajvezeték helyreállítási munkálatai
stories.summary: A Bloomberg értesülései szerint szerdán újra jöhet az olaj Magyarországra.

stories.title: Vučić és Magyar telefonon beszéltek, a szerb elnök meghívta a következő magyar kormányfőt
stories.summary: Energetikai és kisebbségi kérdéseket is érintettek.

stories.title: Cser-Palkovics András közölte, hogy nem mond le
stories.summary: Hiába kérte tőle Magyar Péter, hogy „vonja le a következtetést”, kitölti a ciklusát.

stories.title: Gimnáziumi igazgatókkal egyeztetett Magyar Péter az oktatási rendszer átalakításáról
stories.summary: Forrásaink szerint a megbeszélés során egyértelmű volt, hogy Magyar Péter nem riad vissza a nagyobb léptékű, határozottabb átalakításoktól sem.

stories.title: Valamit valamiért: fákat kellett kivágni a szegedi lézerközpont napelemei miatt
stories.summary: Az árnyékolás rontja a teljesítményt, ezért kellett eltávolítani a fákat, amelyeket minierdővel pótol az ELI-ALPS.

stories.title: From soldier to developer: How German troops are test building their own drones
stories.summary: During the "Grand Quadriga 2026" exercise, the German army has been demonstrating the transformation of modern warfare, where drones, data and speed are increasingly decisive.

stories.title: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.summary: Exclusive: Australian Recreation Union says it plans to support candidates and parties that oppose gun control laws brought in after terror attack Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A gun users’ group targeting marginal Labor seats in next year’s New South Wales election plans to campaign for candidates who oppose the government’s firearm laws enacted after the Bondi terror attack . stories.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
stories.summary: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their ve

stories.title: 7 takeaways from Olly Robbins’ evidence on the Peter Mandelson vetting saga
stories.summary: LONDON — The “Blob” can bite back, it turns out. Days after Keir Starmer sacked him as the Foreign Office’s top civil servant, Olly Robbins, spoke for the first time Tuesday about a row over the vetting of Peter Mandelson to be Britain’s ambassador to the U.S. The row centers on Robbins’ decision to unilaterally grant Mandelson full “developed vetting,” a security clearance, despite a vetting officer recommending it should be denied. Starmer sacked Robbins last Thursday for not telling him of th

stories.title: Russia uses AI to hack Europe, Dutch intelligence warns
stories.summary: Russia is deploying artificial intelligence to accelerate its cyberattacks on Europe, Dutch military intelligence warned Tuesday — and the threat is only expected to grow. “Russian capabilities are growing. Russian actors can execute their cyberattacks at a high pace. This is partly because they can partially automate their attacks, including by means of artificial intelligence,” the Netherlands Defence Intelligence and Security Service (MIVD) said in its annual report for 2025 . The

stories.title: US ‘restricts intelligence sharing with South Korea’ after minister identified suspected nuclear site
stories.summary: Washington reportedly limits satellite data after minister spoke publicly about suspected facility in North Korea The US has partly restricted intelligence sharing with South Korea after the country’s unification minister publicly identified a suspected North Korean nuclear site, according to reports in South Korean media. Chung Dong-young told lawmakers in March that North Korea was operating uranium enrichment facilities in Kusong, a north-western area that had not previously been officially c

stories.title: Two Israeli soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue in Lebanon village
stories.summary: Israel Defense Forces say the ‘soldiers’ conduct completely deviated from IDF orders and value’ Two Israeli soldiers have been removed from combat duty and sentenced to 30 days in jail after one used a sledgehammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon while the other filmed him, the Israel Defense Forces have said. stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US-Iran talks in doubt as JD Vance’s Pakistan trip reportedly on hold
stories.summary: Officials say US vice-president has not yet left Washington for Islamabad after Donald Trump said he did not want to extend ceasefire Trump says US likely to resume bombing Iran as ceasefire nears end JD Vance to lead US delegation in Pakistan if Iran agrees to talks Iran’s armed forces are ready to deliver an “immediate and decisive response” to any renewed hostile action by its adversaries, Ali Abdollahi, commander of the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, was quoted by the Tasnim news age

stories.title: Japan loosens long-standing curbs on arms exports
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

stories.title: Russia faked economic data to appear more resilient to its war and sanctions, intel report says
stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: L’Union européenne cherche à relancer les achats groupés de gaz
stories.summary: BRUXELLES — La Commission européenne souhaite relancer les achats coordonnés de gaz au niveau de l’UE, alors que la guerre au Moyen-Orient suscite des craintes en matière d’approvisionnement, selon une déclaration prérédigée que POLITICO a pu consulter. Il s’agit de l’une des nouvelles mesures envisagées par l’exécutif européen pour protéger contre la hausse des prix les Etats membres, qui s’inquiètent de la perspective d’une pénurie d’énergie. Dans le même temps, cette mesure risque de provoque

stories.title: Florida launches ‘criminal investigation’ into ChatGPT, fueled by university shooting
stories.summary: TALLAHASSEE, Florida — Florida escalated its dispute with OpenAI and the ChatGPT tool Tuesday as the state pursues potential criminal charges tied to the popular bot’s role in a deadly campus shooting at Florida State University last year. State Attorney General James Uthmeier said Florida’s OpenAI probe is expanding because a “criminal investigation is necessary” after state prosecutors determined ChatGPT “offered significant advice” to the suspected FSU shooter. The state issued subpoenas to t

stories.title: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuse The actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine. The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast

stories.title: ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out
stories.summary: An Ecuadorian fishing crew describe their ordeal as victims of Trump’s purported war on ‘narcoterrorists’ By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers, told the Guardian. “Everything wa

stories.title: Kremlin forcing big firms to join ‘witch-hunt’ against internet rebels, claims report
stories.summary: Campaigners say banks and web platforms are being told to collect data on customers visiting blocked sites Major Russian companies have been conscripted into a “witch-hunt” against users trying to circumvent online controls, researchers have said, as the Kremlin continues trying to cut its citizens off from the global internet. Banks and web platforms are collecting data on users of virtual private networks (VPN) tools, which obscure an individual’s real location and allow them to access sites b

stories.title: With the US-Iran ceasefire about to expire, could Trump put boots on the ground?
stories.summary: The US has deployed more forces since the truce and senior Iranians seem to be relishing the prospect of the fight It is the doomsday scenario that Donald Trump repeatedly swore he would never countenance: putting boots on the ground in a deployment that could embroil the US in a Middle East “forever war”. Now, with a two-week ceasefire in the war with Iran coming to an end and prospects for renewed negotiations hanging by a thread, the chances of the president breaking that pledge and ordering

stories.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his frien

stories.title: ‘He talks too much’: how Trump’s erratic commentary is the real block to an Iran deal
stories.summary: US president’s contradictory statements only make Tehran more wary of anything but the most watertight deal Donald Trump’s blend of threats and hubristic commentary, often casually dismissive of Iran, has, as much as the continuation of the US naval blockade of Iranian ports, been a key stumbling block to restarting peace talks between the two countries under Pakistan’s mediation in Islamabad. However much the Iranian foreign ministry insists it will not respond to every social media utterance i

stories.title: Four Labour activists charged with trying to fix candidate selection process
stories.summary: Police bring criminal case over alleged manipulation of party database in Croydon East constituency Four Labour activists have been charged over allegations that a party database was manipulated to increase a candidate’s chance of selection. The four include Joel Bodmer, 40, who ran as a potential candidate for the Croydon East constituency in south London but later withdrew. He is charged with perverting the course of justice for allegedly altering phone records. Continue reading...

stories.title: Push to bring US and Iran together for talks as ceasefire deadline looms
stories.summary: Donald Trump says US ready to renew attacks but Iran appears unwilling to bend to president’s threats Intense efforts are under way to bring Iran and the US together in Pakistan for a new round of talks a day before the end of the two-week ceasefire that has paused the conflict in the Middle East. Donald Trump said on Tuesday he was ready to renew attacks against Iran if progress was not made at any talks, with the US military “raring to go”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Self-serving stuff’: anger remains high in Whitehall over Olly Robbins sacking
stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s decision to oust senior official may have knock-on effect for No 10’s relationship with civil service Fury within Whitehall about the treatment of Olly Robbins remains white hot several days on from Keir Starmer’s decision to sack the senior Foreign Office civil servant. “It’s just total self-serving, narrow, selfish, political-endgame stuff,” said one supporter of Robbins, who was dismissed for failing to tell the prime minister that the now disgraced former US ambassador Peter M

stories.title: EU foreign ministers reject proposal to suspend association agreement with Israel
stories.summary: A part suspension was tabled by Ireland, Spain and Slovenia but did not receive enough backing from other member states The EU remains split on imposing sanctions on Israel, despite some member states criticising the country over the plight of Gaza and violence against Palestinians by Israeli settlers in the West Bank. stories.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
stories.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

stories.title: Russia and North Korea connect road bridge ahead of summer opening, Moscow says
stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.

stories.title: Qatar backs ceasefire as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad
stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: Israeli army jails two soldiers for smashing head of Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had said on Monday he was "stunned and saddened to learn that an IDF soldier damaged a Catholic religious icon in southern Lebanon."

stories.title: German police launch mass raids of 50 premises to smash Syrian trafficking ring
stories.summary: Police believe the ring operated by using genuine residency documents issued to Syrian refugees in Germany, which were sent to people in Syria with a similar appearance.

stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. stories.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
stories.summary: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. Continue reading...

But then the ride-share platform deactivated his account. “It’s even worse than getting belted,” Thorn said. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Human rights campaigners say honour for Avraham Zarbiv endorses ethnic cleansing and war crimes Middle East crisis – live updates An extremist rabbi known for razing civilian homes in Gaza will light a torch at Israel’s independence day celebration on Tuesday, a role human rights campaigners said marked the embrace of genocide as the official “spirit of the nation”. Avraham Zarbiv is one of 14 people chosen for their “extraordinary contribution to society and the state”, alongside a scientist, a

stories.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
stories.summary: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across

stories.title: Airlines demand UK relax noise rules and cut flight tax as fuel shortage looms
stories.summary: Ministers also asked to alter compensation rights and suspend emissions trading scheme amid Middle East war Airlines are lobbying the UK government to relax environmental and noise rules, modify passenger rights and cut taxes on flying, as they prepare for higher costs and a possible shortage of jet fuel because of the war in the Middle East. A list of policy requests submitted to ministers and the aviation regulator includes suspending the emissions trading scheme and relaxing limits on night f

stories.title: California man arrested for allegedly swapping in pasta for Lego pieces then returning them
stories.summary: Irving police department announced bust in pun-filled social media post about alleged $34,000 Lego looting So much for using his noodle. A California man pilfered thousands of dollars in Lego toy sets from the retailer Target in a return-based scam, sometimes swapping valuable figurines with dried pasta pieces and before returning the construction-centric toys, authorities recently alleged. Continue reading...

stories.title: Prolific unregulated sperm donor loses UK legal fight to be named as child’s father
stories.summary: Robert Albon cannot be declared four-year-old’s father because he ran illegal sperm donation business, court rules A prolific unregulated sperm donor described in the high court as a “highly dangerous man” has lost a legal fight to be named as the father of a child conceived using his sperm. Robert Albon, who calls himself Joe Donor, was not entitled to be declared the father of a four-year-old child because he was running an illegal sperm donation business, Britain’s most senior family court ju

stories.title: Bill banning people born after 2008 from buying tobacco clears UK parliament
stories.summary: Ministers hope tobacco and vapes bill, which will become law next week, will create a ‘smoke-free generation’ A bill banning anyone born after 2008 from buying tobacco in the UK has completed its progress through parliament in a move that ministers hope will create a “smoke-free generation”. Under the tobacco and vapes bill anyone born on or after 1 January 2009 will never be able to be legally sold tobacco across the UK, in an effort to save lives and reduce the burden on the NHS. Continue read

stories.title: Germany news: Trial begins in Istanbul over tourist deaths
stories.summary: Six people have been charged with negligent homicide over the deaths of four members of a German family at a hotel in Turkey. Meanwhile, a major climate conference kicked off in Berlin. DW has more.

stories.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
stories.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

stories.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker
stories.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

stories.title: Taiwan blames pressure from China for nixed Africa trip
stories.summary: The Taiwanese government says Beijing used economic coercion to convince three African nations to revoke permission for President Lai Ching-te to traverse their airspace.

stories.title: India news: Delhi braces for first heatwave of the year
stories.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert with temperatures over 40 degrees predicted for the coming days. Meanwhile, reports suggest India and the US are on the verge of a trade deal. DW has more.

stories.title: Nigeria's $2B power bailout tests Tinubu reforms
stories.summary: Nigeria has approved billions to clear power sector debts. Analysts say the move may stabilize electricity supply, but deeper structural problems remain unresolved.

stories.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
stories.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

stories.title: What are Japan, South Korea learning from Hormuz disruption?
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has shaken Tokyo and Seoul, highlighting their dependence on maritime trade for essential goods including food and fuel.

stories.title: UK moves to ban smoking for everyone born after 2008
stories.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

stories.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
stories.summary: Attacks like the last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic around Gulf, Straight of Hormuz
stories.summary: The passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz - the world's busiest oil shipping canal - has been severely disrupted amid the US-Israeli war with Iran. Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic in the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz from April 18 to April 20, amid a fragile US-Iran truce.

stories.title: Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for talks with Iran on hold, official says
stories.summary: The US delegation led by Vance was expected to leave from Washington for Islamabad on Tuesday, which had hosted a first unsuccessful round of talks earlier this month.

stories.title: Iran hangs man accused of ties to Israel and setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests
stories.summary: Iran executes Amirali Mirjafari for allegedly plotting to burn a Tehran mosque and collaborating with Israel and the US, as rights groups decry rising executions.

stories.title: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
stories.summary: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

stories.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
stories.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

stories.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
stories.summary: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

stories.title: Dozens of Turkish coal miners detained after march for unpaid wages
stories.summary: Some 110 Doruk Madencilik workers who had been marching to Ankara to demand unpaid wages and compensation were detained as demands for rights and safety grow louder.

stories.title: Arrests in Nigeria after abduction of local monarch 'held for ransom'
stories.summary: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

stories.title: Mass trial for 486 alleged MS-13 gang members begins in El Salvador
stories.summary: The group are collectively accused of committing more than 47,000 crimes, including murder.

stories.title: Gaza needs more than $71 billion for recovery and reconstruction, EU-UN report says
stories.summary: Gaza would need over $71 billion for recovery after two years of war, EU and UN warned Monday, as damage to homes, hospitals, and schools leaves most people displaced.

stories.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?

stories.title: Ofcom investigating Telegram over child sexual abuse material concerns
stories.summary: The popular messaging service told the BBC in a statement it "categorically denies Ofcom's accusations".

stories.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids

stories.title: Humpback whale Timmy remains stranded in shallow waters in northern Germany
stories.summary: A stranded humpback whale in the Baltic Sea remains stuck near Poel island, Germany, as rescuers struggle to guide it back to deeper waters.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: South Africa's police boss charged in connection with controversial health contract
stories.summary: Fannie Masemola is accused of failing in his duties to provide proper oversight in his role.

stories.title: Iran talks on hold because of Trump’s blockade
stories.summary: Iran is refusing to send its negotiating team to Islamabad to continue talks this week until President Donald Trump lifts the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, according to two Middle East officials briefed on the situation,granted anonymity because they were not authorized to speak publicly. Trump, who hinted on Tuesday that high-level negotiations with Iran would resume “soon,” has given mixed signals about when, or whether, Vice President JD Vance would leave the White House for Pakistan

stories.title: Iranian American woman arrested in Los Angeles for alleged arms trafficking
stories.summary: Federal prosecutor says woman is suspected of dealing weapons to Africa on behalf of Iranian government A California woman was arrested at Los Angeles international airport after allegedly trafficking weapons on behalf of the Iranian government to contacts in Africa, including Sudan. stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. stories.title: Almost 43,000 migrants register in first three days of Spain's regularisation amnesty
stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
stories.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
stories.summary: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump expects bombing to resume if there's no deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said in an interview that the US was ready to resume attacks on Iran unless a deal was struck. A US ceasefire with Iran expires on Wednesday evening ET.

stories.title: South Korea: Police seek to arrest BTS agency chief over fraud allegations

stories.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

stories.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
stories.summary: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

stories.summary: Sátán a Tiszát támogatták. Rém nem kereste Gulyás Gergelyt, Pornóapátiban hangyafasznyival nyert csak a Fidesz.

stories.title: A kávé javítja az ember hangulatát, főleg ha két hétig nem ihatott kávét

stories.summary: A helyzet valamivel jobb, mint amikor a kormány a 480 forintos ársapka idején konkrétan arra kötelezte a töltőállomás-üzemeltetőket, hogy veszteséggel működjenek, de a matek most sem jön ki.

stories.summary: Mészáros Árpád Zsolt örül az igazságszolgáltatásnak, szerinte talán vége lesz a felesleges rongyrázásnak, pénzköltésnek, családon belüli szétszórásnak.

stories.title: Dagad az iráni konfliktus, kemény áremelést jelentett be a világ legnagyobb óvszergyártója
stories.summary: Az óvszerek iránti kereslet az idén mintegy 30 százalékkal emelkedett, mivel sokan nem tartanak otthon eleget.

stories.summary: A Transparency International adatigénylést nyújtott be az elvonulást szervező Államadósság Kezelő Központhoz, így a pontos költségek is kiderültek.

stories.summary: Az időtáv épp egybevág azzal, amin belül a Barátság kőolajvezetéken is megindulhat a szállítás.

stories.title: Nem a hab a lényeg: milyen az eredeti Rákóczi-túrós, és milyen a legjobb?
stories.summary: Nem az a fontos, hogy ki és mikor találta fel, honnan ered a neve ennek a csodálatos süteménynek, hanem hogy melyik eleme a meghatározó. Egy habmentes verziót mutatunk, amelyben a túróé a főszerep, de a habnak is megvan a helye.

stories.summary: Sokan felháborodtak, hogy Rubovszky Rita lehet az oktatási miniszter. Vannak, akik szerint katolikus gyökerei és egyházi iskolai kötődése nem egyeztethető össze a világi oktatással. stories.summary: US strikes on drug boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean have so far killed a minimum of 181 people. Critics have slammed the Trump administration over the strikes, saying they constitute a violation of international law.

In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms

stories.title: Carney says Canada’s strong economic ties to US are ‘weakness’ to be corrected
stories.summary: Prime minister details efforts to attract investment and sign trade deals with other countries in 10-minute video address Canada ’s strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, the country’s prime minister has warned. In a 10-minute video address, Mark Carney spoke about his government’s efforts to strengthen the Canadian economy by attracting new investments and signing trade deals with other countries. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace
stories.summary: Islamabad has seized chance to act as mediator in Iran war and hopes to reap diplomatic and economic benefits As Pakistan works frantically to narrow differences between Iran and the US in its newfound role as global peacemaker, it is also seeking to recast its diplomatic standing and attract business. Pakistani officials, mediating between an unpredictable US president and hardliners in Tehran, were on Monday trying to coax both sides to put the conditions in place for a second round of talks i

stories.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
stories.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

stories.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
stories.summary: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

stories.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
stories.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

stories.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
stories.summary: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

stories.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
stories.summary: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

stories.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
stories.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

stories.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
stories.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

stories.title: Iran pulls out of Islamabad peace talks after US seizes cargo ship
stories.summary: Tehran’s decision comes after the US Navy halted an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, in what was the first interception since Washington instated its naval blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: Carney says Canada must cut reliance on US as tariffs hit economy
stories.summary: The Canadian leader stressed that his country cannot hinge its future on hope that the US will return to the partner it formerly was to his country and that active steps must be taken to protect Ottawa from the disruptions caused by the Trump administration.

stories.title: Oil prices climb as US-Iran stand-off keeps Strait of Hormuz in limbo
stories.summary: Oil prices climbed more than 5% while European markets fell on Monday morning as a stand-off between Iran and the US prevented tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Kites fill the sky as Berck-sur-Mer launches 39th international festival
stories.summary: The 39th International Kite Festival in Berck-sur-Mer fills the sky with giant creations and hosts the World Kite Cup, drawing huge international crowds.

stories.title: Major fire in Malaysia destroys hundreds of homes, displaces thousands
stories.summary: A massive fire in Sandakan, Malaysia, destroyed about 1,000 stilt homes and displaced more than 9,000 people, with survivors now sheltering in temporary camps.

stories.title: Louisiana gunman kills eight children in suspected domestic mass shooting
stories.summary: A man in Louisiana fatally shot eight children, including seven of his own, in an attack on his family on Sunday morning in Louisiana.

stories.title: Obama and Mamdani sing with kids during first meeting in New York
stories.summary: Barack Obama joined New York mayor Zohran Mamdani at a child care centre, reading to preschoolers and backing his affordability agenda for families.

stories.title: Moment US navy fires on and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
stories.summary: US military footage shows a destroyer firing warning shots before boarding and seizing an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it tried to evade a naval blockade, amid heightened tensions near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.title: Community holds vigil after Louisiana mass shooting
stories.summary: Residents gather near the scene of a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana hugging and consoling one another after a father shot dead eight children in a domestic rampage.

stories.title: Japan issues tsunami warning after powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: Authorities have urged residents of the affected areas to seek higher ground, heed safety instructions and exercise caution.

stories.title: Turkey rallies global middle powers in Antalya amid Hormuz crisis
stories.summary: At the Antalya summit, Erdoğan brought together leaders of 150 countries to tackle crises, promote alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz, and showcase the growing diplomatic clout of middle powers.

stories.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
stories.summary: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.

stories.title: Rescuers evacuate hundreds after snowstorm hits Russia’s Far East
stories.summary: Dozens of vehicles were rescued from deep snowdrifts in Zabaikalsky Krai, where over 250 people were taken to shelters after a rare April snowstorm.

stories.title: Nigerian wins global prize for trying to save bats in a country that shuns them
stories.summary: Many Nigerians associate bats with witchcraft but that did not deter ecologist Iroro Tanshi.

stories.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.title: Netanyahu condemns IDF soldier for vandalising Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned the desecration of a statue of Jesus Christ that was carried out by an IDF soldier in a southern Lebanese village.

stories.title: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
stories.summary: The dramatic end to the Delaware Marathon occurred when the lead runner slowed in celebration before a trailing marathoner sprinted toward the finish line .

stories.title: Turkey arrests 90 people with suspected links to so-called Islamic State group
stories.summary: Authorities have not officially connected the arrests to a shootout outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on 7 April in which two police officers were wounded.

stories.title: Police gunfight with favela gang traps 200 tourists on hilltop
stories.summary: The access route to Morro Dois Irmãos was blocked during the operation in Vidigal, leaving scores of alarmed sightseers stuck on the hill.

stories.title: FBI director Kash Patel files $250m defamation suit against The Atlantic
stories.summary: According to The Atlantic, Patel's job as head of the Federal Bureau of Investigation is in jeopardy in part because of what it said were "bouts of excessive drinking" and "unexplained absences".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV holds a giant open air Mass in Angola
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people attended a giant open-air mass by Pope Leo XIV outside the Angolan capital.

stories.title: Trump refuses to lift Hormuz blockade until Iran deal agreed
stories.summary: Trump’s announcement comes after Iran said earlier it would not be sending a delegation to participate in the second round of high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad that were set to take place on Monday.

stories.title: Several killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages – video report
stories.summary: Ukrainian investigators are examining whether a terrorist attack in Kyiv was directed by Moscow, after a man shot dead six people on Saturday before he was killed by police. The gunman, 58, was a Ukrainian citizen born in Moscow. He opened fire on passersby before barricading himself in a supermarket and taking hostages. Detectives sealed off the area in the Holosiivskyi district and tried to negotiate with him. He was killed after a 40-minute standoff Investigators examine whether Ukraine terro

stories.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
stories.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

stories.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
stories.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

stories.title: Labour’s ‘crabwise’ approach to closer EU ties must address damage of Brexit | Heather Stewart
stories.summary: The damage to the economy dwarfs the upsides from the various non-EU trade deals the UK has struck since 2016 Rachel Reeves joined EU finance ministers for dinner in Washington last week, on the sidelines of the International Monetary Fund spring meetings – the first time a chancellor had done so since Brexit. It was the latest symbolic step in Labour’s marked shift towards prioritising closer EU relations. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK seeks EU deals on steel and EVs in push for closer economic ties
stories.summary: Agreements would aim to shield British industry from new steel tariffs and stricter rules on electric vehicles due in 2027 Downing Street hopes to secure deals on steel and electric cars with the EU as it seeks to upgrade the post-Brexit economic relationship. Amid economic uncertainty caused by the conflict in the Middle East and strains in relations with the US, Keir Starmer is seeking closer economic ties with the EU. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.
